page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk operations.

The hardware profile of this instance emphasizes memory capacity over compute core density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads that require substantial memory footprints without the overhead of additional CPU cores. Because there is no bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, users must factor in external network and block storage configurations, which represents a key architectural tradeoff.

The n4d-highmem-16 is designed for memory-intensive applications. Suitable workloads include medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio to prevent out-of-memory errors during execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4d-highmem-16 offers a balanced profile for memory-bound workloads, allowing organizations to scale RAM capacity without paying for unnecessary compute cores. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory allocations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database hosting.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-16 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixteen dedicated vCPUs and a high memory-to-core ratio for demanding data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware
Suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
